title: ACR admin user + pipeline credential sprawl
narrative: Azure Container Registry has admin user enabled and the single shared credential is referenced in 15+ pipeline definitions across Azure DevOps / GitHub Actions. A leak in any one pipeline leaks registry-wide push access.

steps.action: Obtain the admin password from a leaked pipeline log.
Push malicious images under common 'latest' tags; consumers pull.

title: Terraform state in storage account with public access
narrative: Terraform state is stored in a blob container with allowBlobPublicAccess=true. The state file contains every resource ID, every parameter, and often plaintext secrets that Terraform had to 'see' during apply. Anyone with the URL has the entire infrastructure spec.

steps.action: Discover public Terraform state; download state file.
Use harvested secrets to pivot to every named resource.

title: CI/CD service principal with sub-wide Contributor
narrative: The CI/CD deployment SP holds Contributor at subscription scope AND its secret is static. Any PR-triggered workflow has the ability to read/write any resource in the subscription — a malicious PR becomes a subscription takeover.

steps.action: Open a PR that modifies workflow to run attacker code.
SP has Contributor on entire subscription; exfiltrate.

title: GitHub Actions OIDC federation over-scoped
narrative: A federated credential trusts any GitHub workflow in any repository under an organisation — the subject claim is too broad. Any repo in the org can assume the identity. A compromised developer's fork becomes cloud-credentialed code execution.

steps.action: Push malicious workflow to a fork in the org.
Use identity's Azure RBAC to exfiltrate.

title: Azure DevOps PAT with full access + sprawled
narrative: An Azure DevOps PAT is created with Full Access (every scope) and an expiration of 1 year. Developers share it across pipelines, local scripts, and browsers. Any leak is a year-long backdoor to Azure DevOps Repos, Pipelines, and Variable Groups.

steps.action: Harvest the PAT from a dotfile or build log.
Read secrets from variable groups; push malicious commits; trigger pipelines.

title: Deployment workflow without branch protection
narrative: The main branch that deploys to prod has no branch protection — direct pushes allowed, no reviewer required, no status check required. A single compromised dev account can push directly to main and deploy malicious code to prod.

steps.action: git push origin main with backdoor code.
Deploy triggered automatically; backdoor in prod.

title: Deployment slots without configuration isolation
narrative: App Service deployment slots are used for blue-green deploys but app settings are NOT marked slot-specific. A dev staging slot reads prod connection strings. Any vulnerability on the staging slot has prod data at hand.

steps.action: Exploit staging slot which has weaker controls.
Read app settings; includes prod DB string.
steps.gain: Prod data access from staging foothold.

title: Bicep / ARM template storing secret as parameter
narrative: An ARM / Bicep template accepts a storage account key as a plain parameter. Deployment logs in Activity Log record the parameter in cleartext. Anyone with Activity Log reader for 90 days can harvest secrets from past deployments.

steps.action: Parameter 'storageKey' passed in clear to ARM.
Query Activity Log deployments; extract parameter values.

title: Azure DevOps variable group with unencrypted secret + no approval
narrative: An Azure DevOps variable group stores production secrets as plain (not locked) variables. Any user with contributor on the project reads them. There is no approval workflow to access the production variable group.

steps.action: View variable group values in the portal.
Authenticate with stolen secrets to backend systems.

services_at_risk: Every system referenced in the variable group
